meta_title: For Founders: Git with AI to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Git with AI to Solve no portfolio (Checklist)
content.seo_description: Overcome the no-portfolio trap. Use this intermediate guide to build, document, and ship SaaS projects using AI-powered workflows with Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: To overcome a lack of portfolio, build high-utility micro-SaaS tools using an AI-augmented Git workflow. By integrating Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for rapid implementation, you can generate production-grade repositories that demonstrate technical authority, business logic, and deployment expertise, effectively proving your value to potential investors or clients.
content.intro:
The 'no portfolio' trap is the silent killer of founder credibility. You have the vision, the business logic, and the drive, but the market demands proof of execution before they trust you with their capital or their problems. In a world of infinite noise, a blank GitHub profile is a red flag that screams amateurism.

But you don't need years of legacy code to build authority. By leveraging AI-first development workflows, you can compress months of traditional coding into days of high-impact production. We will use Antigravity and Claude Code to turn your conceptual SaaS ideas into a validated, version-controlled portfolio that signals immediate technical competence.

steps.title: Define the High-Value MVP Architecture
steps.description: Before writing code, define a project that solves a specific, painful problem. Use Antigravity to generate a scalable folder structure and schema that reflects enterprise-grade standards.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Architect a clean, scalable Next.js 14 project structure for a micro-SaaS. Include folders for API routes, database models, and server-side actions. Ensure it follows professional SaaS best practices.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Core Business Logic
steps.description: Implement the 'money-making' feature first. Use Claude Code to build the core utility that provides the project's primary value proposition.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust authentication and subscription flow using Stripe API and NextAuth. Focus on secure, modular code.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "Implement Stripe checkout flow with webhook handler for user subscription status"

steps.title: Automate Documentation for Authority
steps.description: A portfolio project is useless if it's hard to read. Generate comprehensive READMEs and technical docs that explain *why* you made specific architectural choices.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a professional README.md for a SaaS project. Include 'Why this architecture', 'Tech Stack', and 'How to deploy' sections to showcase senior-level thinking.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "Update the README with technical design patterns used in this repository"

steps.title: Implement Semantic Git Commits
steps.description: Your commit history is your resume. Use AI to ensure every Git commit is atomic, descriptive, and follows conventional commit standards.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a pre-commit hook script that enforces semantic commit messages for all team members.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "Review recent changes and generate a conventional commit message for the current branch"

steps.title: Ship to Production with Automated CI/CD
steps.description: A portfolio project that isn't live is just a draft. Configure your repo for continuous deployment to demonstrate you know how to operate in production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow for automated testing and deployment to Vercel upon merge to the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "Configure .github/workflows/deploy.yml for Vercel production deployment"

pro_tips: Focus on 'Hard Problems': Don't build another to-do list. Build a tool that integrates with APIs like OpenAI, Stripe, or Plaid to show you can handle complex external dependencies.
The 80/20 Documentation Rule: Spend 20% of your time coding and 80% on the README. A well-documented repo gets 10x more attention from recruiters and investors than a feature-heavy, undocumented one.
Use Branch-Based Development: Even if you are a solo founder, use feature branches. It demonstrates that you understand professional workflows and version control hygiene.
Annotate Your Commits: Go back and edit your commit messages to include the 'Why' behind a change. It transforms your repo into a historical record of your decision-making process.
Add a 'Future Scope' Section: In your README, list technical debt or features you would add if you had more time. It shows you have a product roadmap mindset, not just a coding one.
content.next_steps: Once your first project is live, treat it like a product. Monitor logs, gather user feedback, and create a 'V2' branch. Your next goal is to iterate based on actual usage data rather than theoretical features.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to stop hiding behind an empty profile and start shipping products that generate authority and revenue. Every line of code is an asset—start building your digital legacy today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
